task_desc: Fix update() edge avoidance ordering bug, visibilitychange resume behavior/speed state gaps, and dead code -- (1) edge avoidance ordering: the facingDir exponential interpolation at main.js:1356-1357 runs BEFORE edge avoidance modifies targetDir at main.js:1359-1387, so the fly's facing direction always tracks the pre-avoidance targetDir and the edge correction only takes effect on the NEXT frame; during high-speed states (fly at speed 3.0, startle burst at speed 3.0), this one-frame lag means the fly moves 3px toward a wall before avoidance steering applies, producing more wall collisions and unnecessary touch stimuli that spike fear/groom drives; fix by moving the edge avoidance block (lines 1359-1387) before the facingDir interpolation (lines 1356-1357) so both facingDir and targetDir reflect edge corrections in the same frame, (2) visibilitychange resume state gaps: the resume handler at main.js:252-292 clears stimuli, restores drives, resets drag state and food timestamps (D7.1 + D8.1), but does not reset behavior.current, behavior.startlePhase, speed, or speedChangeInterval; if the fly was in a high-speed transient state when the tab was hidden (fly state: speed up to 3.0, startle burst: speed 3.0), on resume it continues at that speed for up to 500ms until the first brain tick (setInterval restarted at line 291) re-evaluates; during that window the stimuli that triggered the state are cleared but the fly still zooms across the screen, hitting walls and triggering spurious touch stimuli; fix by resetting behavior.current to 'idle', behavior.startlePhase to 'none', behavior.enterTime to Date.now(), speed to 0, and speedChangeInterval to 0 in the resume branch, (3) dead isWalking variable at main.js:931 is assigned in drawFlyBody but never referenced after D3.2 moved walkPhase into updateAnimForBehavior; drawLegs at main.js:1204 declares its own isWalking that is the one actually used; remove the dead declaration. Files: js/main.js.
